title: Flaky Spicy Canadian Fish Poutine
description: A spicy, kid-friendly Canadian dish featuring flaky fish in a rich, cheesy gravy served with crispy fries and spherified onions. This dish is a twist on the classic Canadian comfort food, with a spicy kick and a fun, playful presentation.

ingredients.name: paprika
ingredients.notes: adjust to taste for spice level

ingredients.name: bechamel sauce
ingredients.notes: see instructions for homemade or store-bought.

ingredients.name: frozen french fries
ingredients.notes: thawed and cut to 1/2 inch sticks

instructions: Cut the cod into 1-inch pieces and season with salt, pepper, and paprika.
Dredge the fish pieces in flour, then dust with cornstarch.
Heat butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook the fish until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Transfer the cooked fish to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in the same skillet and add the bechamel sauce. Bring to a simmer and cook until thickened, about 2-3 minutes.
In a separate pot, heat the frozen french fries to 350°F (180°C) and cook until crispy, about 10-12 minutes.
Spherify the onions using sodium alginate and water (see instructions).
